regimeSub: Holiday-thin liquidity with SPX at ATH, yen weakness persists as BOJ remains sidelined.
themes: USDJPY 162.53 → BOJ inaction enabling yen carry revival; Nikkei 69682 benefits from exporter FX tailwind
VIX 16.59 pre-July 4th → vol compression into holiday weekend; dealers short gamma into illiquid tape
Gold $4056 holding gains → real rate ~-6bp (Fed at 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI) keeps hard assets bid despite risk-on
DXY 101.34 soft → Fed at terminal with 2 cuts priced by year-end; EUR strength (1.139) reflects ECB hold

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Fed funds 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = -57bp real; goldilocks for risk but inflation still sticky.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s 4.14% / 10s 4.44% = +30bp steepener; normalization continues, no inversion stress.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: No fresh data but SPX ATH + low VIX implies spreads tight; watch for complacency.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.59 subdued into July 4th; holiday gamma decay favors sellers, risk-on tilt intact.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $70.96 capped; OPEC+ supply fears offset by soft China demand (CSI300 4872 lagging).

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4056 resilient despite equity strength; negative real rates + CB buying sustaining bid.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1552 stable; KOSPI 8084 strong, Korea export cycle healthy, no BOK intervention signals.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: No SOX print but KOSPI 8084 + Nikkei 69682 imply semis bid; AI capex narrative intact.

headline: Risk assets grind to ATHs on holiday-thinned volumes as yen weakness (162.5) and negative real rates (-57bp) sustain the carry/equity bid despite sticky 4.2% CPI.
